item.title: Issue: Missing EntityFrameworkCore.Design NuGet package - @dvojkan
item.description:
It is not stated in tutorial tha NuGet package EntityFrameworkCore.Design should be installed, and due to that, Controllers Scaffolding is failing.

item.title: Issue: Cannot Scaffold movie pages [Part 4]. - @nutchyleo
item.description:
I just followed tutorials to create web apps with .NET 5.0 ( for specific it is v5.0.302) with Microsoft Visual Studio Community 2019 version Version 16.10.3

but after I use the scaffolding tool to produce Create, Read, Update, and Delete (CRUD) pages for the movie model by using "Add MVC Controller with views, using Entity Framework" tool in "Add Scaffold dialog" and followed tutorials to complete MVC Controller with views, using Entity Framework dialog , I got this error showing

(I thought this error coming from "Microsoft.VisualStudio.Web.CodeGeneration")

any advice to let me pass this step ? if you need more information please tell me.

==== Code Generation Log ====

Finding the generator 'controller'... Running the generator 'controller'... Generating a new DbContext class 'MvcMovie.Data.MvcMovieContext' Attempting to compile the application in memory with the added DbContext. There was an error creating a DbContext :(10,19): error CS1001: Identifier expected (11,6): error CS1513: } expected (12,44): error CS8124: Tuple must contain at least two elements. (13,13): error CS1022: Type or namespace definition, or end-of-file expected at Microsoft.VisualStudio.Web.CodeGeneration.ActionInvoker.b__6_0()

(13,27): error CS8124: Tuple must contain at least two elements. at Microsoft.Extensions.CommandLineUtils.CommandLineApplication.Execute(String[] args)

(14,9): error CS1022: Type or namespace definition, or end-of-file expected at Microsoft.VisualStudio.Web.CodeGeneration.ActionInvoker.Execute(String[] args)

(18,5): error CS1022: Type or namespace definition, or end-of-file expected at Microsoft.VisualStudio.Web.CodeGeneration.CodeGenCommand.Execute(String[] args)

(19,1): error CS1022: Type or namespace definition, or end-of-file expected G:\Working_Repo\MvcMovie\MvcMovie\Startup.cs(30,13): error CS8389: Omitting the type argument is not allowed in the current context

========================================

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 1d975a83-1550-b284-d0d8-7fe2ea206ff5
  • Version Independent ID: 48e81b42-7c2b-afbf-71e6-d4f442a4bd1f
  • Content: Part 4, add a model to an ASP.NET Core MVC app
  • Content Source: aspnetcore/tutorials/first-mvc-app/adding-model.md
  • Product: aspnet-core
  • Technology: aspnetcore-tutorials
  • GitHub Login: @Rick-Anderson
  • Microsoft Alias: riande

item.title: Issue: Error when adding migration using Entity Framework Core - @deqing
item.description:
It has following error:

Method 'Create' in type 'Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer.Query.Internal.SqlServerSqlTranslatingExpressionVisitorFactory' from assembly 'Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer, Version=3.1.5.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=adb9793829ddae60' does not have an implementation.

Turned out we need install following two as well

 PM> Install-Package Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer
 PM> Install-Package Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Tools

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 1d975a83-1550-b284-d0d8-7fe2ea206ff5
  • Version Independent ID: 48e81b42-7c2b-afbf-71e6-d4f442a4bd1f

item.title: Issue: Default Hosting Model Seems wrong - @EsraShrouf
item.description:
I have a reason to think there’s a small mistake in it though, regarding the default process hosting model. This https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/host-and-deploy/iis/web-config?view=aspnetcore-5.0&viewFallbackFrom=aspnetcore-3.1 article says it’s in process hosting, however, we experienced otherwise:

We have ASP.NET Core (on .ENT Core 3.1) app hosted on IIS. When hostingModel was not configured in in web.config, we could still see the dotnet process (changed its name and recycled the app pool to make sure also). We even saw events clearly indicating that w3wp.exe process is sending shutdown request to the dotnet process upon recycling the app pool (sorry don’t have the actual logs with me):

Application '….' with physical root '….' shut down process with Id '….' listening on port '….' Process Id: ….. File Version: …. Description: IIS ASP.NET Core Module V2 Request Handler. Commit: ….

If you are on the same page with me, we kindly ask you to please change it. There are also other pages containing the same information regarding in proc being the default, like: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/host-and-deploy/iis/in-process-hosting?view=aspnetcore-5.0&viewFallbackFrom=aspnetcore-3.1

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 5945c30c-0dfb-040e-8bdc-a6c1f06c9931
  • Version Independent ID: f47ff3cc-cd78-d41c-af1c-ef1e752cae88
  • Content: web.config file
  • Content Source: aspnetcore/host-and-deploy/iis/web-config.md
  • Product: aspnet-core
  • Technology: aspnetcore-hostdeploy
  • GitHub Login: @Rick-Anderson
  • Microsoft Alias: riande

item.title: Issue: Default Hosting Model Seems wrong - @EsraShrouf
item.description:
I have a reason to think there’s a small mistake in it though, regarding the default process hosting model. Th article says it’s in process hosting, however, we experienced otherwise.

We have ASP.NET Core (on .ENT Core 3.1) app hosted on IIS. When hostingModel was not configured in in web.config, we could still see the dotnet process (changed its name and recycled the app pool to make sure also). We even saw events clearly indicating that w3wp.exe process is sending shutdown request to the dotnet process upon recycling the app pool (sorry don’t have the actual logs with me):

Application '….' with physical root '….' shut down process with Id '….' listening on port '….' Process Id: ….. File Version: …. Description: IIS ASP.NET Core Module V2 Request Handler. Commit: ….

If you are on the same page with me, we kindly ask you to please change it. There are also other pages containing the same information regarding in proc being the default, like: In-process hosting with IIS and ASP.NET Core | Microsoft Docs

item.title: Issue: Default Process Hosting Model Seems Wrong - @Esra11
item.description:
[Enter feedback here] Th article https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/host-and-deploy/iis/web-config?view=aspnetcore-5.0&viewFallbackFrom=aspnetcore-3.1 says it’s in process hosting, however, we experienced otherwise.

We had an ASP.NET Core (on .NET Core 3.1) app hosted on IIS. When hostingModel was not configured in in web.config, we could still see the dotnet process (changed its name and recycled the app pool to make sure also). We even saw events clearly indicating that w3wp.exe process is sending shutdown request to the dotnet process upon recycling the app pool (sorry don’t have the actual logs with me):

Application '….' with physical root '….' shut down process with Id '….' listening on port '….' Process Id: ….. File Version: …. Description: IIS ASP.NET Core Module V2 Request Handler. Commit: ….

If you are on the same page with me, we kindly ask you to please change it. There are also other pages containing the same information regarding in proc being the default, like: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/host-and-deploy/iis/in-process-hosting?view=aspnetcore-5.0&viewFallbackFrom=aspnetcore-3.1

@Rick-Anderson edit: Esraa is an MSFT FTE

item.title: Issue: Unable to get the database to seed correctly. - @byronwarwick
item.description:
// Using VS Code -- Error at the "Add The Seed Initializer" Step in this tutoriel and have just copied the code into the Program.cs file.

It seems to work only when i comment out the "//using MvcMovie.Data" line in each of the "SeedData.cs" & "Program.cs" files.

Also when told to create the SeedData file might be beneficial to call it SeedData.cs in the tutorial.

Trust this helps.

item.title: Issue: CSP meta tags update - @Ponant
item.description:
In https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/blazor/security/content-security-policy?view=aspnetcore-6.0

I had to add a connect-src to make the blazor wasm work properly in .Net 6 preview 6.

<meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy"
         content="base-uri 'self';
         connect-src 'self' ws://localhost:49773/;
              block-all-mixed-content;
              default-src 'self';
              img-src data: https:;
              object-src 'none';
              script-src https://stackpath.bootstrapcdn.com/
                         'self'
                         'sha256-v8v3RKRPmN4odZ1CWM5gw80QKPCCWMcpNeOmimNL2AA='
                         'unsafe-eval';
              style-src https://stackpath.bootstrapcdn.com/
                        'self'
                        'unsafe-inline';
              upgrade-insecure-requests;">

Otherwise I get the error Refused to connect to 'ws://localhost:49773/BlazorAppWasm/' because it violates the following Content Security Policy directive: "default-src 'self'". Note that 'connect-src' was not explicitly set, so 'default-src' is used as a fallback.

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 88177a7d-c9ff-c245-5629-a462c9258abf
  • Version Independent ID: 6e0b5c52-90a1-5ca6-bfad-df33a8beae6c
  • Content: Enforce a Content Security Policy for ASP.NET Core Blazor
  • Content Source: aspnetcore/blazor/security/content-security-policy.md
  • Product: aspnet-core
  • Technology: aspnetcore-blazor
  • GitHub Login: @guardrex
  • Microsoft Alias: riande

item.title: Issue: Mention easier way of persisting additional tokens 3 - @KurtP20
item.description:
This is in response to #22613. As far as I understand this page, you describe a way of persisting additional claims when using an externel login provider. The way you described is very complex and might be necessary in case of google authentication, I cannot tell. But if AzureAD is used, it is way too complicated. Ticking the right marks in Azure Portal is enough to receive the additional claims in info.Principle.

Maybe I misinterpret the intention of this page then it's fine. When I first read this page, I was overwhelmed by the amount of learning it would take me to receive the additional claims.

So if, in the case of e.g. google authenticaion, it is necessary to establish an authentication scope, manually map the keys and create the claims I would leave the page as it is. At the top of the page however, I suggest to add a note, that if AzureAD is used, it suffices to select the additional tokens in AzurePortal and be done.

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 64faf33b-2511-5e8f-c1b0-94f32dc07fa4
  • Version Independent ID: 2697abc8-7727-2eba-74a5-fbbc8a7c80ff
  • Content: Persist additional claims and tokens from external providers in ASP.NET Core
  • Content Source: aspnetcore/security/authentication/social/additional-claims.md
  • Product: aspnet-core
  • Technology: aspnetcore-security
  • GitHub Login: @Rick-Anderson
  • Microsoft Alias: riande

item.description:
To recreate: Create a new blazor wasm web app project (asp.net core hosted: ticked; pwa: ticked; individual user account selected) on .net5.0, visual studio version 16.10.3. Right-click the server project and publish with your free azure profile.

Result: it never starts, and I didnt change a single line of code. [Enter feedback here]

item.title: Issue: Article gives good overview, but quite some important "details" are missing - @HansGuckInDieLuft
item.description:
I would have had expected to find some hints on when objects in services are explored and subsequently rendered, and how a binding is being set up and can be modified if needed. I found from trial-and-error that there is no execution plan for initialization is being set up within Blazor, instead it reliies on (non-null) objects being found at some early stage (before intialization). Objects that are being instanciated later simply "come too late". There should also be a documentation as to the dependency of the kind of services as well as client- or server-sided Blazor apps.

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: a970fb72-3dd0-d173-585d-2ccdd7923f81
  • Version Independent ID: 4871f4f7-9cf6-ac7a-0f21-c65d34bd1119
  • Content: ASP.NET Core Razor component lifecycle
  • Content Source: aspnetcore/blazor/components/lifecycle.md
  • Product: aspnet-core
  • Technology: aspnetcore-blazor
  • GitHub Login: @guardrex
  • Microsoft Alias: riande

The docs show how to handle a websocket connection in the Startup.cs which seems like very bad design.

It does not say how to handle a websocket request in a controller.

item.title: Issue: Missing Steps at Postman Installation and Post - @tkati
item.description:
Hi you seem to be missing steps on how to start the server for Postman to interact with and complete the request. There doesn't appear to be any mention anywhere to even start a server. While I was able to figure it out what was going on I'd imagine a minority but still good percentage of people encounter unnecessary errors on Postman.

The local server that I ended up getting to work with postman also was a different server tool then what I used for earlier steps, so it added some extra confusion in conjunction with the ports.

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • Microsoft Alias: riande

item.title: Issue: Lifecycle events incomplete / misleading - @GiffenGood
item.description:
From the text and diagram of the lifecyle events section it says that "Call OnInitialized{Async}. If an incomplete Task is returned, the Task is awaited and then the component is rerendered."

Although it is true that the component is rerendered after the await, it is also rendered prior to the await, because StateHasChanged is called prior to the task being awaited. IMO, this should be stated explicitly. If a user doesn't know this they may be confused as to why they are getting null exceptions from rendering when, according to the docs, it should not have taken place yet.

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: a970fb72-3dd0-d173-585d-2ccdd7923f81
  • Version Independent ID: 4871f4f7-9cf6-ac7a-0f21-c65d34bd1119
  • Content: ASP.NET Core Razor component lifecycle
  • Content Source: aspnetcore/blazor/components/lifecycle.md
  • Product: aspnet-core
  • Technology: aspnetcore-blazor
  • GitHub Login: @guardrex
  • Microsoft Alias: riande

item.title: Issue: Please add a note how to enable CSS isolation if it isn't working - @BieleckiLtd
item.description:
Please add a note to stress out that below line is required to enable CSS isolation.

<link href="{ASSEMBLY NAME}.styles.css" rel="stylesheet">

For us migrating from .net core 3.1 it is not added by default.

Document Details

  • Content Source: aspnetcore/blazor/components/css-isolation.md

  • Technology: aspnetcore-blazor
  • GitHub Login: @daveabrock
  • Microsoft Alias: riande

item.title: Issue: Bad link in Net 5.0 Documents "RCL Pages Layout" - @rm-code
item.description:
The link in the section below is invalid in the 5.0 Version of the page.

The 3.0 version of the page the link works as expected: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/razor-pages/ui-class?view=aspnetcore-3.0&tabs=visual-studio#rcl-pages-layout

In the 5.0 version of the page the link needs to be changed from: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/razor-pages/ui-class?view=aspnetcore-5.0&tabs=visual-studio#rcl-pages-layout

To: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/razor-pages/ui-class?view=aspnetcore-5.0&tabs=visual-studio#rcl-pages-layout-2

Document Details

item.title: Issue: insert or add - @caglarsarikaya
item.description:
https://github.com/dotnet/AspNetCore.Docs/blob/9dffb59e759b3eb45c101c55814b7334b2e814ab/aspnetcore/mvc/advanced/custom-model-binding/samples/2.x/CustomModelBindingSample/Startup.cs#L20

hello, sorry but I didn't find any information about ModelBinderProviders. What is different

options.ModelBinderProviders.Insert(0, new AuthorEntityBinderProvider()); or options.ModelBinderProviders.Add(new AuthorEntityBinderProvider());

between them?

https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.web.mvc.modelbinderproviders?view=aspnet-mvc-5.2

item.title: Issue: Trying to Deploy .netcore application on Linux using Nginx - @harikrishnamupp
item.description:
[Enter feedback here]

I am trying to deploy a .netcore app (2.1) on ubuntu 16.04 machine

When i run command dotnet "filename.dll" i am getting below error,

All the dependencies are installed (dotnet runtime, dotnet core sdk 2.1)

code runs fine on IIS

Unhandled Exception: System.Security.Cryptography.CryptographicException: Unix LocalMachine X509Store is limited to the Root and CertificateAuthority stores. ---> System.PlatformNotSupportedException: Unix LocalMachine X509Store is limited to the Root and CertificateAuthority stores. --- End of inner exception stack trace --- at Internal.Cryptography.Pal.StorePal.FromSystemStore(String storeName, StoreLocation storeLocation, OpenFlags openFlags) at System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Store.Open(OpenFlags flags) at Synapse.STS.Startup.ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services) in C:\PATH\Startup.cs:line 77 --- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown --- at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.ConventionBasedStartup.ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services) at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.Internal.WebHost.EnsureApplicationServices() at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.Internal.WebHost.Initialize() at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.WebHostBuilder.Build() at Synapse.STS.Program.Main(String[] args) in C:\PATH\Program.cs:line 38

line 38 from program.cs var host = BuildWebHost(args); Line 77 from startup.cs store.Open(OpenFlags.ReadOnly);

below is the code defined for certificate,

X509Store store = new X509Store(StoreLocation.LocalMachine); X509Certificate2 cer = new X509Certificate2(); try { string tb = _configuration["Settings:SigningCredentialsTP"]; store.Open(OpenFlags.ReadOnly); X509Certificate2Collection col = store.Certificates.Find(X509FindType.FindByThumbprint, tb, false);

Kindly advise

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: dcdd2c35-bf2b-2673-d150-303a8aaa54d2
  • Version Independent ID: 815fe034-c9fc-aee3-a8b5-f2e860b30302
  • Content: Host ASP.NET Core on Linux with Nginx
  • Content Source: aspnetcore/host-and-deploy/linux-nginx.md
  • Product: aspnet-core
  • Technology: aspnetcore-hostdeploy
  • GitHub Login: @Rick-Anderson
  • Microsoft Alias: riande

item.title: Issue: Suggestion to add a code example - @fukalov
item.description:
Code below depends on the NuGet package Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.Google

    services.AddAuthentication()
        .AddGoogle(options =>
        {
            IConfigurationSection googleAuthNSection =
                Configuration.GetSection("Authentication:Google");

            options.ClientId = googleAuthNSection["ClientId"];
            options.ClientSecret = googleAuthNSection["ClientSecret"];
        });

I believe that the code below is necessary as an alternative

	services.AddAuthentication()
		.AddOpenIdConnect("Google", "Google", options =>
			{
				IConfigurationSection googleAuthNSection =
					Configuration.GetSection("Authentication:Google");
					
				options.ClientId = googleAuthNSection["ClientId"];
				options.ClientSecret = googleAuthNSection["ClientSecret"];
				options.Authority = "https://accounts.google.com";
				options.ResponseType = OpenIdConnectResponseType.Code;
				options.CallbackPath = "/signin-google"; 
			})
		.AddIdentityServerJwt();

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 6d19b295-f935-3755-4ca4-9d0717b21e75
  • Version Independent ID: 747a179e-90b7-de07-e270-82414963048a
  • Content: Google external login setup in ASP.NET Core
  • Content Source: aspnetcore/security/authentication/social/google-logins.md
  • Product: aspnet-core
  • Technology: aspnetcore-security
  • GitHub Login: @Rick-Anderson
  • Microsoft Alias: riande

item.title: Issue: Remove duplicate, outdated readme.md file - @steveberdy
item.description: In the documentation for the First MongoDB App Tutorial the markdown file is actively edited. However, there is a duplicate, un-improved version of this file in the 3.x code sample's readme file. This should be deleted.

item.title: Issue: Refactor approach on max file size - @guardrex
item.description:
From @dustout on https://github.com/dotnet/AspNetCore.Docs/pull/22701 ...

Max file size check is limited to 500kb without specifying long.MaxValue in file.OpenReadStream

Remarks ...

I see it now in the API remarks. I agree with the update, but we should go thru an issue for two reasons ... it's not really a "patch" scenario ...

  • There are two sample apps with the code. We moved to sample app-based snippets, and there's a sample app for each hosting model.
  • More importantly, I think we might want to refactor the whole approach into a try-catch scenario that eliminates the conditional check below on the file size given that OpenReadStream will throw if the file size is too large.

Also, I think we can go with the maxFileSize for the limit.

If you want to work on this further, @dustout, let me know. :ear: ... Otherwise, I'll take care of this early next week. Thanks for reporting that oversight ... my bad!

UPDATE: And there's a third reason that this will need more work: The limitation has to be called out in the topic's text as well. We can't count on devs seeing the API doc ... I didn't take it into account. It didn't pop up in testing because I was only working with small test files.

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 5ea474d5-234a-f393-8609-892a2deb5d2e
  • Version Independent ID: c11d981c-05af-c19d-a333-feedd5978639
  • Content: ASP.NET Core Blazor file uploads
  • Content Source: aspnetcore/blazor/file-uploads.md
  • Product: aspnet-core
  • Technology: aspnetcore-blazor
  • GitHub Login: @guardrex
  • Microsoft Alias: riande

item.title: Issue: How to pass arguments into the queued background work - @WhitWaldo
item.description:
The example given, while helpful, doesn't shed much light into how it could be modified to allow arguments to be passed into the queue and stored along the tasks for processing at a later time.

As it is, since the QueueHostedService implements BackgroundService, it neatly handles a no-argument situation since the ExecuteAsync override simply calls BackgroundProcessing and passes in its CancellationToken, but then BackgroundProcessing itself then pulls the function out of the queue to execute, and.. well, there's no argument that's been stored, so passing anything into the await workItem(customArg, stoppingToken) fails for lack of a sourced customArg.

It'd be useful to see a second example provided that explains how to queue data to pass into the ValueTask methods so it's available at processing time.

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: c3460b0d-9c60-3a06-ac63-18cf990e53c3
  • Version Independent ID: b2dece1f-15b8-03af-6d52-7cc070432b4b
  • Content: Background tasks with hosted services in ASP.NET Core
  • Content Source: aspnetcore/fundamentals/host/hosted-services.md
  • Product: aspnet-core
  • Technology: aspnetcore-fundamentals
  • GitHub Login: @Rick-Anderson
  • Microsoft Alias: riande

item.title: Issue: Controller or scoped service as a state in IMemoryCache entry - bad practice? - @AccessViolator
item.description:
Shouldn't this be considered a bad practice? https://github.com/dotnet/AspNetCore.Docs/blob/05b7c5370f0d23db025671224dcc64dd2286eff8/aspnetcore/performance/caching/memory/sample/WebCache/Controllers/HomeController.cs#L95 Controllers are essentially scoped objects and may inject scoped services. This is at least a memory overuse issue - state object can't be garbage collected until cache entry is removed.

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 13f5f8f9-18dd-9c12-9ed1-fcc6b44ba7fd
  • Version Independent ID: 640b4f36-5886-7b69-1c95-cfc8574a02fd
  • Content: Cache in-memory in ASP.NET Core
  • Content Source: aspnetcore/performance/caching/memory.md
  • Product: aspnet-core
  • Technology: aspnetcore-performance
  • GitHub Login: @Rick-Anderson
  • Microsoft Alias: riande

item.title: Issue: Changelog for the Javascript client - @Tiberriver256
item.description:
Hello,

I am upgrading from version 3.1.7 of the javascript client to version 5.0.7. I would like to know what breaking changes were introduced in version 4 and version 5 of the library and ideally some instructions on how to migrate from the older version of the library.

Is there any resource like this available?
